ic_nav_.use: All UI icons — interactive, navigation, actions. DEFAULT for all interface elements.

ic_badge_.use: Illustrative / feature / category icons. Decorative, larger contexts.

ic_nav_ar_.use: RTL Arabic mirrored variants of nav icons. Use when dir=rtl.

ic_badge_ar_.use: RTL Arabic mirrored variants of badge icons.

ic_nav_menu_.use: Bottom navigation bar tab icons. App only.

ic_payment_.use: Payment method logos. Self-contained from fds-payment-badges.css.

rule_twoSizesOnly: FDS uses exactly two icon sizes: 24px (nav) and 48px (badge). NEVER set font-size to any other value. Adjust wrapper/container for layout space — not the icon.

fdsIcons.covers: All ic_nav_, ic_badge_, ic_alerts_, ic_inline_, ic_flightindicator_ icons

codeShare.covers: 190 airline partner logos (.ic-badge-{iata}). SVGs inlined as data URIs.

countryFlags.covers: 236 country flags (.ic-nav-flag-{iso2}). SVGs inlined as data URIs.

paymentBadges.covers: 100 payment method logos (.ic-payment-{method}). SVGs inlined as data URIs.

iconGapProtocol.instruction: If no FDS icon exists for a required use case, NEVER use a third-party icon or emoji. Instead:
steps: Flag: 'Icon gap: [description] — no FDS equivalent exists'
Use the closest available FDS icon as a temporary placeholder
Suggest a name following FDS naming convention (ic_nav_{name} or ic_badge_{name})
Recommend raising with the FDS design system team

ic_nav_.use: Primary UI actions, wayfinding, system controls

ic_nav_ar_.use: RTL-mirrored nav icons for Arabic layouts

ic_badge_ar_.use: RTL-mirrored badge icons for Arabic layouts

ic_alerts_.use: Status indicators: tick, warning, error, info

ic_inline_.use: Inline with text — external link, expand, gallery, 360/3D

ic_flightindicator_.use: Flight status: confirmed, delayed, cancelled, rescheduled

ic_app_.use: App store buttons: App Gallery, Google Play, App Store, QR App

Ic_nav_menu_.use: Social media icons: Apple, Facebook, X, Instagram, YouTube, Snapchat, WhatsApp

_note: Icon components are not stored here as discrete component entries. Icons are identified by class name prefix + name. See commonNavIcons above for the most-used set. For the full catalogue, read foundations.md §Iconography.

allIcons.name: ic_badge_mobile_phone
allIcons.description: A mobile phone icon used as a badge or indicator to represent phone-related features, connectivity, or contact methods within the Flow Design System. Use in headers, contact sections, or settings panels to visually denote phone functionality or mobile-specific actions.

allIcons.name: ic_badge_computer_desktop
allIcons.description: Represents desktop computing hardware, workstation setups, or fixed-location computer access. Use in device management, hardware selection, and office or home computing contexts.

allIcons.name: ic_badge_television
allIcons.description: Represents television or streaming service features, content availability, or device connectivity. Use in entertainment settings, media device management, and content source selection.

allIcons.name: ic_badge_3d_360_vr
allIcons.description: Indicates content or features that are immersive, three-dimensional, or offer a panoramic or virtual reality experience. Use to tag media types, highlight compatible devices, or filter content in technology platforms, media libraries, and gaming interfaces.

allIcons.name: ic_nav_menu_notification
allIcons.description: Three-line menu icon with notification dot

allIcons.name: ic_nav_language
allIcons.description: World globe in left-pointing speech bubble

allIcons.name: ic_nav_ar_language
allIcons.description: World globe in right-pointing speech bubble

allIcons.name: ic_nav_replace_arrows
allIcons.description: A navigation icon depicting two arrows swapping positions, used to indicate a replace, exchange, or swap action in a user interface. Commonly used in booking flows, data tables, or settings to allow users to switch between two items, seats, or options.

allIcons.name: ic_nav_ar_multitrip
allIcons.description: An icon showing two overlapping arrows forming a loop, representing multiple trips or a round trip itinerary.

allIcons.name: ic_nav_special_luggage
allIcons.description: Icon of a suitcase with a star or special marker, used to indicate special luggage items like fragile, oversized, or priority baggage.

allIcons.name: ic_payment_amex_safekey
allIcons.description: Ameex authentication for secure online payments.
